ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Proof that there is a God.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: That there is a God, then, is no matter of doubt to those who receive the Holy Scriptures, the Old Testament, I mean, and the New; nor indeed to most of the Greeks.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For, as we said, the knowledge of the existence of God is implanted in us by nature.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But since the wickedness of the Evil One has prevailed so mightily against man’s nature as even to drive some into denying the existence of God, that most foolish and woe-fulest pit of destruction (whose folly David, revealer of the Divine meaning, exposed when he said, The fool said in his heart, There is no God), so the disciples of the Lord and His Apostles, made wise by the Holy Spirit and working wonders in His power and grace, took them captive in the net of miracles and drew them up out of the depths of ignorance to the light of the knowledge of God.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In like manner also their successors in grace and worth, both pastors and teachers, having received the enlightening grace of the Spirit, were wont, alike by the power of miracles and the word of grace, to enlighten those walking in darkness and to bring back the wanderers into the way.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But as for us who are not recipients either of the gift of miracles or the gift of teaching (for indeed we have rendered ourselves unworthy of these by our passion for pleasure), come, let us in connection with this theme discuss a few of those things which have been delivered to us on this subject by the expounders of grace, calling on the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: All things, that exist, are either created or uncreated.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: If, then, things are created, it follows that they are also wholly mutable.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For things, whose existence originated in change, must also be subject to change, whether it be that they perish or that they become other than they are by act of will.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But if things are uncreated they must in all consistency be also wholly immutable.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For things which are opposed in the nature of their existence must also be opposed in the mode of their existence, that is to say, must have opposite properties: who, then, will refuse to grant that all existing things, not only such as come within the province of the senses, but even the very angels, are subject to change and transformation and movement of various kinds?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the things appertaining to the rational world, I mean angels and spirits and demons, are subject to changes of will, whether it is a progression or a retrogression in goodness, whether a struggle or a surrender; while the others suffer changes of generation and destruction, of increase and decrease, of quality and of movement in space.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Things then that are mutable are also wholly created.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But things that are created must be the work of some maker, and the maker cannot have been created.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For if he had been created, he also must surely have been created by some one, and so on till we arrive at something uncreated.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Creator, then, being uncreated, is also wholly immutable.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And what could this be other than Deity?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And even the very continuity of the creation, and its preservation and government, teach us that there does exist a Deity, who supports and maintains and preserves and ever provides for this universe.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For how could opposite natures, such as fire and water, air and earth, have combined with each other so as to form one complete world, and continue to abide in indissoluble union, were there not some omnipotent power which bound them together and always is preserving them from dissolution?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: What is it that gave order to things of heaven and things of earth, and all those things that move in the air and in the water, or rather to what was in existence before these, viz., to heaven and earth and air and the elements of fire and water?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: What was it that mingled and distributed these?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: What was it that set these in motion and keeps them in their unceasing and unhindered course?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Was it not the Artificer of these things, and He Who hath implanted in everything the law whereby the universe is carried on and directed?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Who then is the Artificer of these things?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Is it not He Who created them and brought them into existence.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For we shall not attribute such a power to the spontaneous.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For, supposing their coming into existence was due to the spontaneous; what of the power that put all in order?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And let us grant this, if you please.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: What of that which has preserved and kept them in harmony with the original laws of their existence?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Clearly it is something quite distinct from the spontaneous.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And what could this be other than Deity?
